ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for Fish Tikka Pizza. FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category. The dish is a Turkish-style flatbread topped with spiced marinated fish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Pizza) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for fish tikka pizza or Turkish fish flatbread pizza in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for fish tikka pizza. SNOMED CT does not have a specific concept for this fusion dish.

main_ingredients: tikka spice marinade (yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, ginger, garlic)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one whole fish tikka pizza weighing around 300 grams, typically served as a main course at a Turkish or Lebanese restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite: Pizza with seafood topping (SR Legacy) and tikka-marinated fish components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Fish Tikka Pizza exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component analysis: pizza dough base (~40% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 'Pizza crust, prepared from recipe'), white fish tikka topping (~35%, USDA SR Legacy 'Fish, cod, Atlantic, cooked, dry heat' adjusted for tikka marinade with yogurt and spices), tomato sauce (~10%, USDA SR Legacy 'Tomato sauce, canned'), mozzarella cheese (~10%, USDA SR Legacy 'Cheese, mozzarella, whole milk'), and vegetables/herbs (~5%). Weighted average per 100 g calculated from these components. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and flatbread-based foods — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza (cheese pizza, GI ~60)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Fish Tikka Pizza. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza on a refined wheat base (GI ~60, Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The fish tikka topping does not substantially alter the GI relative to the dough base, which dominates the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 73.5) / 100 = 44.1, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 24.5 × 3 = 73.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1260 mg per full pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a pizza and avoid additional high-sodium condiments, as a full serving provides approximately 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) and high glycemic load per full serving (~44)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion (150 g) to reduce glycemic load to approximately 22, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese (~3.2 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium, partially offset by omega-3-containing fish
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may benefit from the omega-3 fatty acids in the fish component, but should limit portion size to manage saturated fat and sodium intake, and request reduced-cheese preparation where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Tikka Pizza is a fusion dish served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants in Singapore, combining the South Asian technique of tikka marination with a Turkish-style flatbread or pizza base. The fish — typically a firm white fish such as cod, snapper, or hammour — is marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture containing cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, ginger, and garlic, then grilled or baked before being placed on a thin pizza base spread with tomato sauce. The pizza is finished with mozzarella cheese, sliced onions, bell peppers, and fresh herbs such as parsley or coriander, then baked in a wood-fired or conventional oven. The result is a dish with a crisp, chewy base, tender and aromatic spiced fish, and a mild dairy richness from the melted cheese. Nutritionally, it provides a moderate protein contribution from the fish (~12.5 g per 100 g), moderate carbohydrate from the dough base (~24.5 g per 100 g), and meaningful micronutrients including vitamin B12, selenium, and phosphorus from the fish. The sodium content is elevated (~420 mg per 100 g), making portion control relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), driven primarily by the refined wheat dough base. Clinically, the dish is suitable for most adults in moderate portions, with the fish component providing beneficial lean protein and omega-3 fatty acids. It is halal-certified in the context of Sultan Palace restaurant in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fish tikka pizza?
What fish is used in fish tikka pizza?
What is the glycemic index of fish tikka pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Fish Tikka Pizza exists in any approved primary or secondary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for pizza crust, cooked white fish, tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and vegetables. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza (GI ~60). Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and documented, but direct measurement data for this specific dish is unavailable.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; no direct measurement exists for this dish in any approved database. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the component sources used for estimation and cannot be reliably disaggregated from the composite. However, all -1 fields in this record have been replaced with estimated values where defensible component data was available; no -1 sentinel values remain in this record. 2. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database cheese pizza data; the fish topping does not substantially alter the GI relative to the wheat dough base. 3. Glycemic load per serving = (60 × 73.5) / 100 = 44.1, based on a 300 g serving containing 73.5 g carbohydrate. 4. The dish is served at Sultan Palace (Boat Quay) in Singapore and is classified as halal in that restaurant context. 5. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this fusion dish. 6. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore. Turkish romanized name also provided. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from component USDA SR Legacy data and are not confirmed zero values; all are positive estimates.
